message:
So my friend texted me at 6 PM last Tuesday: 'THE POWER'S OUT. WHAT DO I DO???' in all caps, naturally. I could practically hear the panic through my phone.

I talked her down—literally just walked her through it: grab the flashlight from under the kitchen sink (yes, she had one, buried behind cleaning supplies), charge her phone with the power bank she'd forgotten about, and raid the fridge before it gets warm.

Twenty minutes later she was fine. Actually... fine. Not thrilled, but fine.

Turns out when you have a couple of basics within arm's reach, a blackout goes from EMERGENCY ALERT SOUND to just... an inconvenience. No drama, no scrambling in the dark, no stress-eating all the ice cream (okay, maybe a little of that).

I sent her a little refresher on what actually helps during an outage, and honestly? It took the edge off for her next time.

Do you have one of those friends who goes full panic mode, or are you that friend? 😄
